# Makefile with clean targets

A well-written Makefile includes a clean target for each category of build artifact:

```makefile
.PHONY: clean clean-deps clean-build clean-all

# Remove compiled output only
clean-build:
	rm -rf dist/ build/ .next/ out/

# Remove dependency directories
clean-deps:
	rm -rf node_modules/

# Remove Python bytecode
clean-pyc:
	find . -name "__pycache__" -type d -exec rm -rf {} +
	find . -name "*.pyc" -delete

# Remove all generated files (but not node_modules by default)
clean: clean-build clean-pyc
	rm -rf .cache/ .parcel-cache/ coverage/

# Nuclear option: restore to pristine checkout state
clean-all: clean clean-deps
```

Usage:

    make clean        # fast: just build artifacts
    make clean-all    # slow: also reinstalls dependencies

Each target has a precise, bounded scope. No target references paths outside the project directory.
